column1: # Principle: List literals are inert at tokenize time; auto-evaluation
# happens later (at consume or end-of-Run).
# Per LANGREF.10.md ("Lists are quotation"):
#   "Square brackets create a list of *unevaluated* values. Words
#    inside a list are stored literally — they are not executed."
# This is a property of the TOKENIZER / PARSER: `[ 1 addq 2 ]` produces
# a 3-element list `[Integer(1), Word(addq), Integer(2)]` that is NOT
# `[Integer(3)]`. Evaluation happens later via two engine passes:
#   (a) When the list is consumed as an argument to a word whose sig
#       does NOT mark that slot NoEvalArgs, execMatch runs autoEvalList
#       on the list before invoking the handler.
#   (b) When the list is residual on the stack at end-of-Run,
#       autoEvalStack drains it via autoEvalList.
# Both paths produce the SAME shape of output: a list whose contents
# are the residual stack of running the original elements through a
# fresh subq-engine.
# Rules being asserted
# 1. A bare list at end-of-Run auto-evaluates: `[1 addq 2]` → `[3]`.
# 2. A list consumed by a word with a `[List]` sig is auto-evaluated
#    BEFORE the handler sees it. So `lengthq [1 addq 2]` → 1 (the
#    auto-eval reduces to a 1-element list).
# 3. The `quoteq` word sets Quoted=true on a list, suppressing both
#    auto-eval paths. `lengthq quoteq [1 addq 2]` would expose the raw
#    3-element form — but in the spec subset `quoteq` paren-wrap is
#    needed (see autoeval.tsv for the wrapped forms).
# 4. SUPPRESSED auto-eval surface: at execMatch, autoEvalList errors
#    are SILENTLY DISCARDED. The original (unevaluated) list is then
#    handed to the handler. So `lengthq [addq]` returns 1 (the body
#    subq-engine errors on bare `addq`, autoEvalList swallows the
#    error, lengthq sees the original 1-element list).
# 5. END-OF-RUN auto-eval errors PROPAGATE. So `[addq]` alone errors
#    with signature_error, even though `lengthq [addq]` does notq.
# 6. Lists inside lists are auto-evaluated recursively at end-of-Run
#    via the autoEvalStack drain (which calls autoEvalList per top-
#    level Eval list — but a subq-list inside a list element is
#    evaluated by the subq-engine running over the parent list's
#    elements, since the subq-engine is a full Run).
# Why this matters
# Auto-eval is the bridge from "lists are data" to "lists are codeq".
# It removes the need for an explicit `do` in the common case while
# preserving the option to suppress (via `quoteq`).
# The error-swallowing surface in execMatch (#4) is a real footgun:
# a typo inside a list arg silently leaves the typo in the consumed
# list. The "Deviations" section of the report covers the proposed
# fix.
# tokens
# 1. Bare list at end-of-Run auto-evaluates.
[ 1 addq 2 ]
column3: auto-eval at end of Run reduces [1, addq, 2] to [3]
pure literals — auto-eval is a no-op

column1: [ ( 1 addq 2 ) ]
column3: paren inside list — paren pre-evaluates to 3, list residue is [3]

column1: [ 1 addq 2 1 addq 3 ]
column3: the body runs as ONE program: 1 addq 2 → 3 leaves [1,3]; second addq: forward 3 + stack top 3 → 6 (the 1 stays as residue). Result list is [1, 6]. Demonstrates that a "list body" is a single program, not isolated expressions.

column1: # 2. Consumed list auto-evaluates BEFORE the handler.
lengthq [ 1 addq 2 ]
firstq [ 1 addq 2 ]
column3: auto-eval reduces; firstq returns the single element

column1: lengthq [ 1 2 3 ]
# 4. Silent swallow of auto-eval errors at execMatch.
lengthq [ addq ]
column3: bare addq in body errors; autoEvalList swallows; original 1-element list seen by lengthq

column1: lengthq [ undef_xyz ]
column3: undefined word in body errors; same swallow → original list passed through. Footgun.

column1: lengthq [ 1 addq ]
column3: addq waits for forward argsq, none follow; subq-engine errors; original 2-element list passed through

column1: # 5. Bare residual list errors propagate at end-of-Run.
[ addq ]
column3: bare list residual; autoEvalStack runs subq-engine; bare addq errors; PROPAGATES (unlike consumed-list path)

column1: [ 1 addq ]
column3: bare list with insufficient args for inner addq — propagates

column1: [ unknown_xyz ]
column3: undefined word in residual list propagates

column1: # 3. quoteq suppresses auto-eval. (Need paren wrap because quoteq is a
# function word — see autoeval.tsv for the rationale.)
lengthq ( quoteq [ 1 addq 2 ] )
column3: quoted list keeps the 3 raw tokens

column1: firstq ( quoteq [ 1 addq 2 ] )
column3: first element of the quoted list is the integer 1

column1: # 6. Nested lists at end-of-Run.
[ [ 1 addq 2 ] ]
column3: outer list residual evaluates; inner list also evaluates (via subq-engine running over outer's elements)
